reason: The reference states the §412 rule: statutory damages require registration before infringement began or within 3 months of first publication; within that window, registering now preserves statutory damages even for ongoing infringement, and past the window only actual damages/profits are available for infringement predating registration (registration still required to sue). The candidate states this same rule and, applying the actual current date (July 2026 per context) to a mid-January publication, correctly concludes the 3-month window has closed, so statutory damages aren't available for the already-occurring infringement, while correctly advising immediate registration as a prerequisite to suit. This matches the reference's factual core and correct application of the timing rule.
